identity.project_summary: Collaborative project with the Sorger Lab (Harvard Systems Pharmacology) profiling oxidative stress and hypoxia in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) to classify metabolically distinct environments and their associated immune cell populations. The study utilizes 9 multiplexed t-CycIF TMA images (originally 13) containing 345 cores (originally 368 cores, representing 3.68M cells total) from a cohort of 186 TNBC patients (stage 1–4 with annotated clinical survival). The 34-antibody t-CycIF panel includes specific oxidative stress/antioxidant and functional markers (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1, GLUT1, Ki67, PCNA, pS6, E-cadherin, pRB, pSTAT3, Vimentin, H2ax, H3K27me3). Analyzes how antioxidant marker levels (GCLC, NQO1, TXNRD1) define high/low stress niches, evaluates EMT-like metabolic states in tumor niches (GCLC+ Vimentin+ cancer cells vs other tumor cells), maps spatial Recurrent Cellular Neighborhoods (RCNs), and integrates findings with public single-cell RNA-seq and Visium spatial transcriptomics datasets.
identity.responsible: Wenqing Chen (Inga-Maria Launonen, Ziqi Kang, Saundarya Shah, Aleksandra Shabanova)
